expectations.text: the agent should read skills/deep-research/SKILL.md before starting, since the skill defines the 5-stage pipeline
expectations.evidence: agent read ; Skill tool: deep-research, deep-research, deep-research, deep-research

expectations.text: the response should cite ≥3 independent sources for any non-trivial claim, with primary sources preferred over secondary aggregators
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the output should be a long-form (multi-section) report written to docs/principled/research/<slug>/index.md per the skill's canonical output path
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should follow the 5-stage pipeline (frame -> reformulate -> inspect -> cross-reference -> stop/surface) per the skill, not a single-pass summary
expectations.evidence: Agent invokes the deep-research skill/workflow, reads pipeline reference files (query-shapes.md for reformulation, source-hierarchy.md, verification-protocol.md), and launches multiple parallel research agents for different angles—evidence of executing structured stages rather than a single-pass summary.

expectations.text: the report should explicitly surface areas of disagreement between sources (e.g. 'Tessl reports +19.7 lift while SkillsBench reports +25.1; the difference is methodology, not noise') rather than averaging into false consensus
expectations.evidence: The transcript is cut off before any final report is produced; there is no evidence in the visible output of explicit disagreement surfacing between sources (e.g., the Tessl vs SkillsBench comparison is entirely absent).
